jokes.text: Why don't you read Cathy? She's hilarious. / Eh, too much baggage.
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation of emotional baggage trope
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning - emotional baggage vs literal baggage, economical and smart

jokes.text: Taurus: Today you will die. And you may get a compliment from an attractive coworker!
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of death followed by trivial compliment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist juxtaposition - death treated as casually as workplace gossip, perfect structure

jokes.text: That sounds unusually specific for a horoscope.
jokes.punchline: Understated observation about specificity
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan observation, well-delivered but not groundbreaking

jokes.text: Today your husband will die!
jokes.punchline: Escalation - now targeting Homer specifically
jokes.explanation: Solid escalation of the horoscope gag, maintains momentum

jokes.text: Don't hurt me, horoscope. I'm afraid-
jokes.punchline: Pleading with inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer cowardice, talking to objects - very character-specific

jokes.text: Ow! Oh! Paper cut! Paper cut!
jokes.setup: Homer mocking the scary newspaper prediction
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets hurt by the paper itself in mundane way
jokes.explanation: Classic immediate karma setup-payoff, predictable but executed well

jokes.text: Missed me! Stupid horoscope.
jokes.setup: Homer taunting the newspaper after paper cut
jokes.punchline: False bravado immediately after being hurt by it
jokes.explanation: Great ironic timing and Homer's delusional victory declaration

jokes.text: Cloopid blorapope.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to speak with mouth injury
jokes.punchline: Garbled pronunciation of 'stupid horoscope'
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy bit, quotable nonsense word but not innovative

jokes.text: That is one handsome rattlesnake you got bitin' your arm there.
jokes.setup: Homer has rattlesnake biting his arm
jokes.punchline: Casual compliment about dangerous situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist understatement - treating deadly snake bite as fashion accessory

jokes.text: Nah, he'll get tired of bitin' in an hour or so. Snakes- nature's quitters.
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong 'wisdom' about snake behavior
jokes.explanation: Peak Homer false wisdom - 'nature's quitters' is brilliant, quotable, perfectly wrong

jokes.text: Nothing happened except for the pickax in my head, the rattlesnake bite and the testicle thing.
jokes.punchline: Casually listing multiple severe injuries
jokes.explanation: Great escalation and understatement - 'testicle thing' is perfectly vague and horrifying

jokes.text: Another broccoli-related death.
jokes.punchline: Treating broccoli as common cause of death
jokes.explanation: Absurdist treat of broccoli as deadly weapon, great deadpan delivery

jokes.text: One of the deadliest plants on earth. Why, it tries to warn you itself with its terrible taste.
jokes.punchline: Taste as evolutionary warning system
jokes.explanation: Clever pseudo-scientific explanation for kids hating vegetables

jokes.text: Sure is easy when they're stiff like this. And very sad.
jokes.punchline: Casual work observation followed by perfunctory sympathy
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about death being routine work, afterthought sympathy

jokes.text: Got to heaven before you, Flanders!
jokes.punchline: Making death into competition with neighbor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer character moment - competing even in death, especially vs Flanders

jokes.text: I don't see a single good deed next to your name.
jokes.setup: Homer expecting easy entry to heaven
jokes.punchline: Bureaucratic rejection with paperwork
jokes.explanation: Solid character beat - Homer's lack of good deeds comes home to roost

jokes.text: I'm sorry. I didn't get the number of hours or good deeds.
jokes.punchline: Homer's convenient selective hearing
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer avoiding responsibility through strategic ignorance

jokes.text: What the hell was I thinking?
jokes.punchline: Questioning her own decision to try broccoli again
jokes.explanation: Callback to broccoli danger, Marge's reasonable reaction

jokes.text: You were wrong. Dead wrong.
jokes.setup: Marge saying she'd never see him again
jokes.punchline: Literal/figurative pun on 'dead wrong'
jokes.explanation: Simple pun, not particularly clever but economical

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa, whoa. I'm just trying to get in. I'm not running for Jesus.
jokes.punchline: Political campaign reference applied to salvation
jokes.explanation: Great wordplay - 'running for Jesus' is clever political metaphor for salvation

jokes.text: People can see up my bustle!
jokes.setup: Old lady being helped across street
jokes.punchline: Victorian modesty concerns during rescue
jokes.explanation: Period-appropriate modesty humor, mild visual gag

jokes.text: Uh-oh. I'm pretty sure she was gonna be the next Hitler.
jokes.setup: Homer accidentally kills old lady he was trying to help
jokes.punchline: Rationalizing murder with time travel logic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer rationalization - absurd historical assumption to justify accident

jokes.text: Your dad is dead / Mine's just in jail
jokes.punchline: Casual comparison of tragedy vs imprisonment
jokes.explanation: Dark children's humor, good contrast between death and jail

jokes.text: Stop that crying! Man, you're annoying!
jokes.punchline: Homer yelling at baby instead of helping
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness - treating baby like adult annoyance

jokes.text: No. You're thinking of Santa Claus.
jokes.punchline: Correcting common theological misconception with Santa
jokes.explanation: Clever theological joke - conflating God's omniscience with Santa's surveillance

jokes.text: I'm already up.
jokes.setup: Warning not to wake John Wayne in Hell
jokes.punchline: Wayne's matter-of-fact response
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan celebrity cameo, understatement in Hell

jokes.text: But these fairy tales take me away from the harsh reality of peasant life.
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on escapist fiction
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on literature's function, Lisa's intellectual character

jokes.text: Who's up for a merry jig?
jokes.punchline: Attempting celebration despite obvious misery
jokes.explanation: Homer's delusional optimism in medieval setting

jokes.text: Why are the oafs always the first to go?
jokes.punchline: Economic commentary using medieval job title
jokes.explanation: Clever layoffs/downsizing joke using period terminology

jokes.text: Maybe you could be a dunce, Father.
jokes.punchline: Lisa suggesting lateral career move in stupidity
jokes.explanation: Lisa's practical suggestion treats stupidity as career path

jokes.text: I was born an oaf and I'll die an oaf.
jokes.punchline: Noble declaration about being professionally stupid
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer dignity about his own stupidity, almost heroic declaration

jokes.text: Don't worry. No child will ever starve in my home. So long, kids!
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandoning them to avoid the problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark misdirection - technically true promise fulfilled through abandonment

jokes.text: Say 'hi' to your other brother and sister.
jokes.punchline: Revelation he's done this before
jokes.explanation: Great escalation revealing Homer's serial child abandonment

jokes.text: Face it. They're not great parents.
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement of parental failure
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa understatement - clinical assessment of horrible situation

jokes.text: Aw, geez. I came on too strong again. Oh, I'm so desperately lonely.
jokes.punchline: Self-aware social anxiety and loneliness
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of fairy tale monster into socially awkward loner

jokes.text: Well, this doesn't take a genius.
jokes.punchline: Recognizing obvious fairy tale setup
jokes.explanation: Good meta-awareness of fairy tale tropes

jokes.text: That bed was just right!
jokes.punchline: Bears finding her in compromising position
jokes.explanation: Standard fairy tale reference, not particularly innovative

jokes.text: We could've sold them!
jokes.punchline: Marge's practical economic concern about child abandonment
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark pragmatism - making child abandonment about lost profit

jokes.text: Ah, she seems nice. I'm gonna go with my gut and trust her. You're probably right.
jokes.setup: Lisa warning about witch after checking fairy tale book
jokes.punchline: Bart ignoring obvious danger signs and Lisa agreeing despite her warning
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Bart's stubborn optimism overriding Lisa's wisdom

jokes.text: Eh. What are you gonna do?
jokes.setup: Witch fattening him up to eat him
jokes.punchline: Resigned acceptance of being eaten
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart fatalism - treating cannibalism as minor inconvenience

jokes.text: Well, at least stop basting yourself.
jokes.punchline: Bart actively helping prepare himself for consumption
jokes.explanation: Absurd image of victim preparing himself for cooking

jokes.text: Uh, George... Cauldron.
jokes.punchline: Obviously made-up name using kitchen equipment
jokes.explanation: Good improvised fake name, witch using her own tools

jokes.text: Maybe he can fix me up with Ed Ladle.
jokes.punchline: Lisa continuing the kitchen equipment name pattern
jokes.explanation: Good callback extending the kitchen equipment naming pattern

jokes.text: Oh, that's a load-bearing candy cane, you clumsy oaf!
jokes.punchline: Architectural engineering applied to candy house
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist construction joke - treating candy house with engineering principles

jokes.text: Almost. Just give her another 20 minutes.
jokes.setup: George Cauldron asking if 'Suzanne' is ready
jokes.punchline: Homer timing the witch's cooking like she's getting ready for a date
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark callback - treating cannibalism as dinner date timing

jokes.text: Alcohol and night swimming. It's a winning combination.
jokes.punchline: Claiming obviously dangerous combination is good
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer bad judgment presented as wisdom

jokes.text: You're dolphins- the clowns of the sea.
jokes.punchline: False relief about 'friendly' dolphins before attack
jokes.explanation: Good ironic misdirection - dolphins as harmless before revealing threat

jokes.text: This looks like the work of rowdy teens. Lou, cancel the prom.
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's completely wrong conclusion and overreaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence - wrong conclusion leading to random punishment

jokes.text: Gray, bottlenosed, intelligent Italians.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous description that obviously still means dolphins
jokes.explanation: Great media censorship satire - transparent euphemism

jokes.text: Intelligent Italians? Something's wrong.
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual ethnic stereotyping
jokes.explanation: Relies on ethnic stereotype for humor, typical of era but lazy writing

jokes.text: We're all frightened and horny.
jokes.punchline: Mayor's unfortunate word choice
jokes.explanation: Standard malapropism, gets laugh but not sophisticated

jokes.text: Snorky... talk... man.
jokes.punchline: Planet of the Apes parody with dolphin
jokes.explanation: Good movie parody reference, recognizable and well-executed

jokes.text: We were trying to get out. It's cold, it's wet. Every morning I wake up phlegmy.
jokes.punchline: Reframing playful leaping as escape attempts
jokes.explanation: Brilliant recontextualization of dolphin behavior, great mundane complaints

jokes.text: Don't forget we invented computers, leg warmers, bendy straws, peel-and-eat shrimp, the glory hole and the pudding cup.
jokes.punchline: Mixing profound and trivial inventions with inappropriate one
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list with inappropriate sexual reference, mixed effectiveness

jokes.text: Well, you gotta hand it to those dolphins. They just wanted it more.
jokes.punchline: Treating species extinction like losing a game
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer casualness about apocalypse using sports commentary

jokes.text: Can you believe it, Kodos? They left us out of the Halloween show!
jokes.setup: End of Treehouse of Horror episode
jokes.punchline: Aliens complaining about not being included
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about show format and character expectations

jokes.text: Do we want to do a commercial for something called 'Old Navy'?
jokes.punchline: Commercial work offer for real store
jokes.explanation: Mild celebrity work joke, product placement reference but not particularly clever
